title: Applied Causal Inference
summary: Working through the causal inference literature with implementations in Julia and Python. DoubleML, time-series causal models, instrumental variables.
description_long: Dual-language causal methods codebase with tests and validation architecture. Implements DoubleML, instrumental variables, and time-series-aware variants in both Julia and Python. The intent is reference-quality implementations with explicit validation against synthetic data and known answers.
whats_next: Currently dormant; reference-quality state. Future direction: add additional estimators and integrate with the temporal-validation utilities.

title: double_ml_time_series
summary: Companion code + manuscript-style material for temporal partially-linear DML, cross-fitting, HAC inference, and synthetic examples.
description_long: Implementation and methodology notes for double machine learning extended to time-series settings. Covers temporal partially-linear DML, cross-fitting protocols that respect time ordering, and HAC-style inference. Synthetic examples illustrate where naive cross-fitting fails and what to do instead.
whats_next: Manuscript-style write-up in progress. Next: consolidate the cross-fitting variants and publish a worked example notebook.

title: TemporalValidation / temporalcv
summary: Temporal validation utilities and methodology lineage. Walk-forward splits, purging, embargo windows, and leakage prevention.
description_long: Utilities for temporally-aware cross-validation: walk-forward splits with explicit gaps, purging adjacent observations, embargo windows, and other leakage-prevention patterns. The methodology lineage notes connect classical temporal CV practice to modern uses in causal and ML pipelines.
whats_next: Reference-quality state. Future: package as installable Python/Julia modules with consistent API.

title: Prompt-injection PoC
summary: Methodology proof-of-concept for detecting prompt-injection attacks against LLMs. Rigorous evaluation with bootstrap CIs on lift over baselines.
description_long: DeBERTa-v3 with LoRA, evaluated against a baseline ladder (heuristic → logistic regression → frozen encoder → fine-tuned) on locked holdout splits with bootstrap confidence intervals on lift. Calibration via temperature scaling; reliability and ECE reported on a model card. Framed explicitly as methodology proof-of-concept, not a production detector.
whats_next: V2 methodology complete; finalizing DECISIONS.md (D1-D29 recorded). Next: archive the PoC and migrate methodology patterns into the eval-toolkit harness.

title: eval-toolkit
summary: Reusable binary-classification evaluation harness: baseline ladder, bootstrap CIs, calibration, stop-gates against overengineering.
description_long: Pre-v1 evaluation library for binary-classification AI/ML systems. Encodes the methodology used in prompt-injection-detector as reusable components: baseline ladders, bootstrap confidence intervals on metrics and lift, calibration tooling, and explicit stop-gates that fire before adding model complexity.
whats_next: Currently in Round 8 of audit; Decision Y.2 is the stop-gate for cutting v1.0. Next: lock v1.0 API and publish to PyPI.

title: prompt-injection portfolio
summary: Broader pre-alpha field log: ongoing prompt-injection research, attack catalogs, eval scenarios beyond the PoC's scope.
description_long: A pre-alpha companion to the prompt-injection PoC. Wider in scope: tracks attack categories, eval scenarios, OOD probes, and methodology variations that don't fit inside the PoC's locked evaluation protocol. Field-log format — assumes the reader has context.
whats_next: Pre-alpha. Direction: select the most-mature scenarios and migrate them into the eval-toolkit + PoC pipeline.

title: DLAI Study Notes
summary: Online book corpus synthesized from deeplearning.ai short courses. Per-course .tex → MDX conversion with audit-during-conversion content quality pass. Searchable, math-rendered, with Anki flashcards.
description_long: In-progress corpus of study notes synthesized from DeepLearning.AI short courses. Each course is converted from LaTeX source through a quality-audit MDX conversion into a searchable, math-rendered, citation-tracked online book. Includes BibTeX bibliography integration, Pagefind search, KaTeX math, and Anki .apkg deck export. Built on book-scaffold-astro. Currently local-only — publishing surface pending.
whats_next: PoC for a broader pattern: 10-20 Gold-tier DLAI courses targeted; additional course-derived note projects from other sources (e.g., Bertsekas RL+Control, ssm-foundations chapters) lined up. Deploy to a public URL is the immediate-next step.

title: rl_and_control — RL + Control Theory guide
summary: RL + Control Theory from fundamentals to convergence. 28-week structured syllabus, Python/JAX/Julia experiments, 135-paper indexed bibliography, 12 method-family dossiers. Companion to ssm-foundations.
description_long: In-progress authored guide bridging classical optimal control (Bellman, DP, MPC, LQR/LQG) with modern reinforcement learning (policy gradient, actor-critic, model-based, MPC-RL hybrids). 28-week syllabus organized into 3 parts: RL Foundations (10 weeks) -> Control Theory (8 weeks) -> Convergence (10 weeks). Includes Python (PyTorch/Gymnasium/SB3), JAX (Brax, differentiable MPC), and Julia (ControlSystems.jl, JuMP MPC) experiment tracks. Bibliography: 135 papers indexed across 18 thematic sections with status tracking and 12 method-family dossiers.
whats_next: Local-only; publishing surface (Astro/MDX web guide) planned, matching the ssm-foundations book-scaffold-astro pattern. Bibliography (rl_and_control/references/paper_index.md) is the data source for the /lab/research-graph/ demo (shipped 2026-05-24, densified 2026-05-25).

external_context.label: Bertsekas — Reinforcement Learning and Optimal Control (2019)

title: ssm-foundations
summary: 17-chapter lens-led foundations book bridging numerical analysis and dynamical systems to modern sequence-model architectures (SSMs, Mamba, S4, Hyena, DeltaNet). Successor to a 21-week research-guide effort. Ch 1–6 shipped (linear ODEs, stability, linear algebra, discretization, stability regions, symplectic integration).
description_long: Public foundations book derived from the post-transformers research repo. 17 chapters organized lens-first: continuous-time dynamics → discretization → stability theory → structured state-space models → selective scans (Mamba, S4, S4D) → hybrids. Each chapter pairs prose with JAX and Julia companions, reproducible figures, and Julia stability tests. Built on book-scaffold-astro v4 academic preset; deployed via Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets.
whats_next: 6 of 17 chapters implemented (foundations layer complete). Next: Ch 7 (HiPPO theory) and the structured-SSM layer (S4, S4D, Mamba-1/2/3).
